steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles in any triangle add to $180^\circ$. In this triangle, the two given angles are $31^\circ$ and $87^\circ$, so those two values and the unknown angle must total $180^\circ$.
$$31^\circ+87^\circ+T=180^\circ$$

steps.title: Subtract the known angles
steps.content:
First combine the two given angle measures, then subtract that sum from $180^\circ$ to find the remaining angle.
$$31^\circ+87^\circ=118^\circ$$
$$T=180^\circ-118^\circ=62^\circ$$
The measure of the missing angle is $62^\circ$. The answer is A.
